<findings>

[observation] [info] High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected
[observation] [info] Cohesion of 1.0 reflects that root files share no import edges, which is structurally correct for config/script files and not a quality concern.
[observation] [info] Zero coupling confirms the spawn-only architecture is holding: no orchestration script imports domain packages as libraries.
[move-file] [warning] File "scripts/hench-callgraph-analysis.mjs" is pinned to zone "Monorepo Root" but lives in scripts/ — consider moving to ./ to align physical location with architectural zone
[pattern] [warning] 4 of 6 files in monorepo-root are web package files (landing.ts, landing.test.ts, boundary-check.test.ts, build-output-contract.test.ts) — dissolving this misclassification by pinning these files to their correct zones would make monorepo-root a clean orchestration-scripts-only zone.
[relationship] [info] monorepo-root has zero coupling to all other zones, correctly reflecting that orchestration scripts spawn child processes rather than importing domain packages; this invariant should be verified by the architecture-policy e2e test on every PR.
[anti-pattern] [warning] The spawn-only boundary for orchestration scripts (cli.js, web.js, ci.js) is enforced only at the source-file level by architecture-policy.test.js. No test validates that the built or executed artifacts preserve this boundary — a bundler misconfiguration could silently inline domain package code into orchestration scripts without tripping any CI check.
[anti-pattern] [warning] packages/web/src/landing/landing.ts and packages/web/tests/unit/landing/landing.test.ts are Preact page components and their tests, but both are algorithmically grouped into monorepo-root because their import neighbors are sparse. They should be pinned to web-viewer-application. Similarly, packages/web/tests/integration/boundary-check.test.ts and build-output-contract.test.ts are web-package integration tests that should be pinned to their respective test zones, not left in a zone named after the monorepo root.
[suggestion] [warning] Zero zone pins exist in the manifest despite extensive documentation of required pin operations across multiple analysis passes. The misclassifications inflating monorepo-root file counts and distorting web-dashboard coupling metrics cannot self-correct — applying the documented pins should be treated as a blocking prerequisite for trusting any zone health metric as an actionable signal.
[suggestion] [warning] boundary-check.test.ts enforces web package internal sub-layer import rules but is classified in monorepo-root. Pin it to web-viewer-application or a dedicated web-integration-tests zone so zone-level test coverage metrics correctly attribute web internal boundary enforcement to the web package rather than the monorepo root.
[suggestion] [info] scripts/parse-gateway-exports.mjs and scripts/hench-callgraph-analysis.mjs are undocumented developer scripts with no tests or ownership signal. Audit them for current utility: if still needed, add a comment header documenting their purpose and invocation; if obsolete, remove them to prevent the scripts/ directory from becoming a graveyard of unmaintained tooling.

</findings>

<insights>

- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected
- High cohesion (1.0) with zero coupling is expected for a root zone composed entirely of config and orchestration files that don't import each other.
- The orchestration scripts (cli.js, web.js, ci.js, config.js) define the four-tier spawn-only boundary — keeping them at the root prevents accidental library-level imports.
- 31 files is a reasonable footprint for a monorepo root; no bloat concern given the mix of config, docs, and entry-point scripts.
- Zero coupling confirms the spawn-only architecture is holding: no orchestration script imports domain packages as libraries.
- Cohesion of 1.0 reflects that root files share no import edges, which is structurally correct for config/script files and not a quality concern.
- File "scripts/hench-callgraph-analysis.mjs" is pinned to zone "Monorepo Root" but lives in scripts/ — consider moving to ./ to align physical location with architectural zone
- The monorepo import graph decomposes into two structurally separate subgraphs: (1) all non-web zones (monorepo-root, rex-runtime-data, project-documentation, hench-agent, e2e, rex sub-zones, sourcevision sub-zones, llm-client) form isolated islands with zero cross-zone coupling, and (2) the web package zones form the only connected subgraph with active cross-zone imports. This means all architectural coupling risk in the monorepo is concentrated entirely within the web package.
- 4 of 6 files in monorepo-root are web package files (landing.ts, landing.test.ts, boundary-check.test.ts, build-output-contract.test.ts) — dissolving this misclassification by pinning these files to their correct zones would make monorepo-root a clean orchestration-scripts-only zone.
- monorepo-root has zero coupling to all other zones, correctly reflecting that orchestration scripts spawn child processes rather than importing domain packages; this invariant should be verified by the architecture-policy e2e test on every PR.
- The spawn-only architectural invariant (orchestration scripts must not import domain packages) is enforced by architecture-policy.test.js, but the test operates on source files — it cannot detect if a future build step or transpilation adds implicit imports to the dist/ output of cli.js, web.js, or ci.js. The invariant has no production-artifact enforcement layer.
- The spawn-only boundary for orchestration scripts (cli.js, web.js, ci.js) is enforced only at the source-file level by architecture-policy.test.js. No test validates that the built or executed artifacts preserve this boundary — a bundler misconfiguration could silently inline domain package code into orchestration scripts without tripping any CI check.
- packages/web/src/landing/landing.ts and packages/web/tests/unit/landing/landing.test.ts are Preact page components and their tests, but both are algorithmically grouped into monorepo-root because their import neighbors are sparse. They should be pinned to web-viewer-application. Similarly, packages/web/tests/integration/boundary-check.test.ts and build-output-contract.test.ts are web-package integration tests that should be pinned to their respective test zones, not left in a zone named after the monorepo root.
- The sourcevision manifest contains zero zone pins (confirmed: manifest has no 'pins' key and the zoneOverrides map is empty), despite multiple enrichment passes documenting specific files that require pinning. All misclassification artifacts described in previous insights remain structurally unresolved because the pin mechanism has not been applied — zone health metrics for web-dashboard, monorepo-root, and related zones continue to be unreliable as architectural signals.
- Zero zone pins exist in the manifest despite extensive documentation of required pin operations across multiple analysis passes. The misclassifications inflating monorepo-root file counts and distorting web-dashboard coupling metrics cannot self-correct — applying the documented pins should be treated as a blocking prerequisite for trusting any zone health metric as an actionable signal.
- boundary-check.test.ts enforces web package internal sub-layer import rules but is classified in monorepo-root. Pin it to web-viewer-application or a dedicated web-integration-tests zone so zone-level test coverage metrics correctly attribute web internal boundary enforcement to the web package rather than the monorepo root.
- scripts/parse-gateway-exports.mjs and scripts/hench-callgraph-analysis.mjs are undocumented developer scripts with no tests or ownership signal. Audit them for current utility: if still needed, add a comment header documenting their purpose and invocation; if obsolete, remove them to prevent the scripts/ directory from becoming a graveyard of unmaintained tooling.
- [call graph] 484 internal calls, 0 outgoing, 0 incoming (cohesion: 1, coupling: 0)
